name: Cannoli Siciliana
recipeIngredient: 5 tbsp Marsala wine (or any fortified wine)
12 Metal cannoli tubes (cannoli forms may be made from 1-inch aluminum tubing cut into 5-inch lengths if commercial forms are not found)
2 cups Ricotta cheese, as fresh as possible
1/4 cup Candied fruit (citron, orange peel, etc.), finely diced
1/4 cup Semi-sweet chocolate, cut into fine dice (optional)
1 tsp Vanilla extract OR Orange flower water (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour and salt into a bowl. Make a well in the center and add the wine and sugar. Work the flour into the center until you have a firm dough, similar to noodle dough.
2. If the dough is too crumbly, add more wine; do not let it become sticky. Place dough on a lightly floured pastry cloth or board and knead and pound vigorously for about 15 minutes. It should be perfectly smooth by then.
3. Form the dough into a ball, wrap in a clean, slightly dampened cloth, and let it rest for 2 hours.
4. When rested, roll the dough on the pastry cloth or board until very thin (no more than 1/16 inch).
5. Cut the dough into 5-inch squares and roll each square around the metal tubes as follows: place a square diamond fashion with one point toward you. Place the tube vertically across the middle of the diamond, each end at a point of the dough. Fold the sides over the tube, moistening the overlap with water and pressing together.
6. When all squares are rolled, place them a few at a time into deep fat preheated to 375°F. Remove when golden brown (use tongs as they are fragile) and let drain on absorbent paper.
7. When cool, slip off the tubes. Press the ricotta through a fine sieve into a bowl, then beat it until creamy and smooth.
8. Beat in the remaining ingredients with a large fork. Fill a pastry bag fitted with a large plain tube (or use the nozzle without a tube) and pipe the filling into the cooled shells.
9. Makes 12 cannoli.
